title: Sole for footwear with positioning faces
abstract: There is provided a sole for footwear with a bottom surface comprising: a primary surface and one or more positioning face, wherein: the one or more positioning faces are at an angle to the primary surface; engaging a ground with the primary surface causes a wearer of the footwear to take a natural stance; engaging the ground with the one or more positioning faces causes the wearer to take one or more athletic stances; and the wearer switches between the natural stance and the one or more athletic stances by shifting their weight.
claims:
Claims Claims: 1. A sole for footwear, the sole comprising a bottom surface, the bottom surface comprising: a primary surface; and one or more positioning faces; wherein the one or more positioning faces are at an angle to the primary surface; engaging a ground with the primary surface causes the footwear to provide a natural stance; and wherein engaging the ground with the one or more positioning faces causes the footwear to be positioned in one or more athletic stances. 2. The sole of claim 1 , wherein the one or more positioning faces comprise a first positioning face and a second positioning face. 3. The sole of claim 2, wherein the first positioning face is located under a big toe of the wearer. 4. The sole of claim 2, wherein the second positioning face is located under the ball of the wearerâs foot. 5. The sole of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the positioning faces comprise an additional third positioning face. 6. The sole of claim 5, wherein the third positioning face is located under an arch of the wearerâs foot. 7. The sole of any one of claims 1-6, wherein any of the one or more positioning faces are subdivided into subfaces. 8. The sole of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the athletic stances comprise a first athletic stance and a second athletic stance, wherein: the first athletic stance engages the ground with a first set of one or more positioning faces or subfaces; and 

 the second athletic stance engages the ground with a second set of one or more positioning faces or subfaces. 9. The sole of any one of claims 1-8, wherein the athletic stances result in one or more of the wearerâs ankles remaining in a neutral position. 10. The sole of any one of claims 1-9, wherein the one or more positioning faces engages with a transition mechanism to transition between the athletic stance and the natural stance. 1 1. The sole of claim 10, wherein the transition mechanism comprises a hinge and an outsole, such that the hinge is located in between the primary surface and the one or more positioning faces; and the outsole extends from the hinge towards at least one positioning face such that a space is created between the at least one positioning face and the outsole. 12. The sole of claim 1 1 , wherein the space between the positioning face and the outsole is filled with a compressible material. 13. The sole of claim 1 1 , wherein the transition mechanism comprises at least one of a lever, a wedge and a flap. 14. The sole of any one of claims 1-13, wherein the bottom surface is partially covered with a cushion. 15. The sole of any one of claims 1-14, wherein the bottom surface comprises a tread. 16. The sole of any one of claims 1-15, wherein the bottom surface comprises flexible grooves. 17. The sole of any one of claims 1-16, wherein any one or more of the positioning faces is revealed when a positioning face attachment member is detached from the bottom surface. 18. A plate for footwear, the plate comprising: a bottom surface, the bottom surface comprising a primary surface; and one or more positioning faces; wherein the one or more positioning faces are at an angle to the primary surface; a mechanism for attaching the plate to a bottom surface of a shoe; wherein engaging a ground with the primary surface of the plate causes the footwear to provide a natural stance; and wherein engaging the ground with the one or more positioning faces of the plate causes the footwear to be positioned in one or more athletic stances. 19. The plate of claim 18, wherein the mechanism for attaching comprises any of: a Velcro mechanism, mechanical fasteners, zippers, magnets, embedding or a combination of these mechanisms. 20. A method of fixing defects in a userâs natural gait comprising: Conducting a gait analysis; Analysing results of the gait analysis; Creating a customized sole for footwear, the sole comprising a bottom surface, the bottom surface comprising: a primary surface; and one or more positioning faces; wherein the one or more positioning faces are at an angle to the primary surface; engaging a ground with the primary surface causes the footwear to provide a natural stance; and wherein engaging the ground with the one or more positioning faces causes the footwear to be positioned in one or more athletic stances.
